frameworks.name: Normalization Model of Attention (NMoA)
frameworks.one_line: Divisive-normalization account of attentional gain, here extended with a stimulus-independent or direction-tuned normalization term.

frameworks.name: response gain vs input gain
frameworks.one_line: Contrasting psychometric signatures of feature-based attention on neural and behavioral tuning.

core_question: How does feature-based attention to motion direction shape psychometric performance, and can the Normalization Model of Attention account for the observed gain pattern?
key_assumptions: Feature-based attentional effects on neurons translate into analogous psychometric signatures
Transparent motion displays isolate direction-selective attentional modulation
Normalization governs the interaction between attended and unattended features
manipulations: Cued attended motion direction in transparent-motion displays
Width of the attended motion-direction range (narrow vs broad)
key_findings: Feature-based attention produced a mixture of input gain and response gain rather than pure response gain
Narrow attended direction ranges yielded larger input-gain effects than broad ranges
interpretations: Standard NMoA must be extended with a stimulus-independent attentional contribution to normalization or direction-tuned normalization to fit these data
